outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Why a weakened Iran is insisting on prolonging the war
outlets.summary: Analysis of how Iran's strategy focuses on outlasting coalition despite military losses.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities in a major escalation that rattles markets
outlets.summary: Brent hit $119; Gulf energy infrastructure under sustained attack from both sides.

outlets.name: MSNBC
outlets.headline: Gabbard dodges nuclear threat question — again
outlets.summary: House Intelligence Committee hearing produces same evasion as Senate.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Cracks emerge in GOP over $200 billion Iran war funding request
outlets.summary: Some Republicans pushing back on open-ended war spending without clear strategy.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran's Supreme Leader 'misfunctioning' as nation faces new Trump threat
outlets.summary: Live coverage highlights Iran regime weakness and Trump's South Pars warning.

outlets.name: Fox News Opinion
outlets.headline: Operation Epic Fury proves Middle East experts wrong
outlets.summary: Opinion piece argues pre-war skeptics underestimated US military capability.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: F-35 takes hit from Iran — first ever hostile damage to stealth jet
outlets.summary: Coverage of historic F-35 incident focuses on pilot safety and investigation.

outlets.name: Newsweek
outlets.headline: Trump issues new threat to blow up Iran's South Pars gas field
outlets.summary: Trump's Truth Social threat to 'massively blow up' South Pars if Qatar attacked again.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Could oil hit $200 a barrel? Analysts no longer think it's far-fetched
outlets.summary: Wood Mackenzie and Goldman Sachs warn of $150-$200 oil if Gulf infrastructure attacks continue.

outlets.headline: Six Western allies ready to help ensure Hormuz safe passage
outlets.summary: UK, France, Germany, Japan declare willingness to support Hormuz operations.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Iran war: What is happening on Day 20
outlets.summary: Day 20 overview: F-35 hit, Haifa refinery, UNSC resolution, oil $119 spike.

outlets.summary: European markets react to energy supply disruption as Gulf attacks continue.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: A Billion Shekels a Day: The Number That May Decide When Israel's War Ends
outlets.summary: Analysis of Israel's war costs and fiscal constraints that could force a timeline.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Netanyahu says Iran can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles
outlets.summary: Liveblog coverage of Netanyahu evening press conference and its implications.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF kills 20+ Hezbollah fighters, issues Zahrani evacuation
outlets.summary: IDF expanding Lebanese front operations south of Zahrani River.

outlets.name: i24NEWS

outlets.summary: Coverage of Bandar Anzali strike targeting Iran-Russia supply lifeline.

outlets.name: PressTV
outlets.headline: Iran calls the shots: Mearsheimer says US Navy powerless to control Strait of Hormuz
outlets.summary: Iranian state media features American scholar arguing US cannot break Hormuz blockade.

outlets.headline: Iran demands reparations, warns US responsible for all consequences
outlets.summary: Araghchi declares reparations demand and rejects ceasefire conditions.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Resistance forces strike Gulf targets in response to aggression
outlets.summary: Iranian perspective frames Gulf attacks as defensive retaliation.

outlets.headline: US F-35 hit by Iranian fire forced to emergency land
